shared_properties: Project core palette keeps primary #535353, background #F7F7F7, and accent #FF0000.
Canvas target stays 600x150px with pixel-perfect presentation at 60 FPS.
All painted edges keep a 2px outline rhythm and flat shading.
differences: The label is centered and much larger than the live score widget.
The text uses the fail-state phrase GAME OVER in one line.
The art remains static with no fade or shake baked into the image.

context.usage: Centered fail-state title that appears after collision and before restart input.

shape.silhouette: Single centered wordmark reading GAME OVER with blocky 24px pixel lettering.

acceptance_criteria: The phrase GAME OVER remains legible at 1x scale with all letters fully contained inside the frame.
Only #535353 and transparency appear in painted pixels.

reference_images.label: t_rex_runner_sprite_sheet_2x
reference_images.purpose: Grounds score glyph spacing and the game-over text treatment used in the HUD.

reference_images.label: t_rex_runner_sprite_sheet_1x
reference_images.purpose: Grounds score glyph spacing and the game-over text treatment used in the HUD.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: ImageBundle backed by a nearest-neighbor texture.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer in the Loading state and store the handle in a typed resource before gameplay starts.
runtime_notes: Anchor the HUD to whole pixels in the top-right or screen-center layout zone.
Keep all glyph quads on nearest-neighbor sampling to preserve the retro mono-space look.
Expose the atlas handle through a HUD resource so score and restart states can swap visibility without reallocating textures.
